search_queries_used: site:link.springer.com 10.1186/s41038-019-0175-y hypertrophic scar keloid diagnosis wound boundary
candidate_sources_rejected: scar-treatment reviews without consensus or guideline methodology
automatic JSS score and treatment algorithm content
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Narrative reviews were not used when the exact primary consensus document was available.
The legacy workflow lacks the complete scale inputs, and no score, treatment, procedure, medicine, dose, or referral was mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with a clinician-suspected or established hypertrophic scar after injury, surgery, burn, inflammation, or another wound; pediatric, pregnancy, burn, and contracture contexts need additional evidence.
setting_applicability: UAE primary care, dermatology, plastic surgery, burn, wound, and scar follow-up.
UAE_applicability: International scar consensus is relevant to a diverse UAE population, but local specialist pathways, skin-pigmentation context, procedure availability, consent, and referral policy remain required.
recency_verification: The primary publisher full text published in 2019 was reviewed on 2026-07-14; material recency uncertainty remains.
superseded_check: No newer Japan Scar Workshop consensus replacing the 2018 document was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact scar diagnostic section supports documenting a possible or established hypertrophic scar, its onset and progression, growth relative to the original wound, location and clinician-observed morphology without assuming that every raised scar is hypertrophic.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing mature scars, hypertrophic scars, keloids, and mimics; documenting scar growth within or beyond the original wound, shape, elevation, redness, hardness, progression, risk context, visual inspection, palpation, and diagnostic uncertainty.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact assessment points support recording clinician-assessed severity, subjective symptoms, functional effect, lesion location and prior response; no score, diagnosis, treatment, or referral is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording a clinician-established scar type, subjective symptoms, lesion location, severity context, prior response, movement or tension context, and completed specialist assessment without mapping any treatment instruction.

unresolved_source_gaps: Original injury or procedure, exact date, measurements, colour, thickness, firmness, pruritus, pain, bleeding, ulceration, discharge, inflammation, contracture, movement restriction, family and prior-scar history, wound complications, prior treatment, photography, investigations, diagnosis, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported unless separately clinician documented.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
